fields.name: CEN
fields.description: Counter enable Note: External clock, gated mode and encoder mode can work only if the CEN bit has been previously set by software. However trigger mode can set the CEN bit automatically by hardware.

fields.name: UDIS
fields.description: Update disable This bit is set and cleared by software to enable/disable UEV event generation. Counter overflow/underflow Setting the UG bit Update generation through the slave mode controller Buffered registers are then loaded with their preload values.

fields.name: URS
fields.description: Update request source This bit is set and cleared by software to select the UEV event sources. Counter overflow/underflow Setting the UG bit Update generation through the slave mode controller

enum_values.name: AnyEvent
enum_values.description: Any of counter overflow/underflow, setting UG, or update through slave mode, generates an update interrupt or DMA request

enum_values.name: CounterOnly
enum_values.description: Only counter overflow/underflow generates an update interrupt or DMA request

fields.name: OPM
fields.description: One-pulse mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Counter is not stopped at update event

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Counter stops counting at the next update event (clearing the CEN bit)

fields.name: DIR
fields.description: Direction Note: This bit is read only when the timer is configured in Center-aligned mode or Encoder mode.

fields.name: CMS
fields.description: Center-aligned mode selection Note: It is not allowed to switch from edge-aligned mode to center-aligned mode as long as the counter is enabled (CEN=1)

enum_values.name: EdgeAligned
enum_values.description: The counter counts up or down depending on the direction bit

enum_values.name: CenterAligned1
enum_values.description: The counter counts up and down alternatively. Output compare interrupt flags are set only when the counter is counting down.

enum_values.name: CenterAligned2
enum_values.description: The counter counts up and down alternatively. Output compare interrupt flags are set only when the counter is counting up.

enum_values.name: CenterAligned3
enum_values.description: The counter counts up and down alternatively. Output compare interrupt flags are set both when the counter is counting up or down.

fields.name: ARPE
fields.description: Auto-reload preload enable

fields.name: CKD
fields.description: Clock division This bit-field indicates the division ratio between the timer clock (tim_ker_ck) frequency and the dead-time and sampling clock (t DTS )used by the dead-time generators and the digital filters (tim_etr_in, tim_tix),

fields.name: UIFREMAP
fields.description: UIF status bit remapping

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: No remapping. UIF status bit is not copied to TIMx_CNT register bit 31

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Remapping enabled. UIF status bit is copied to TIMx_CNT register bit 31

fields.name: DITHEN
fields.description: Dithering enable Note: The DITHEN bit can only be modified when CEN bit is reset.

fields.name: CCPC
fields.description: Capture/compare preloaded control Note: This bit acts only on channels that have a complementary output.

enum_values.name: NotPreloaded
enum_values.description: CCxE, CCxNE and OCxM bits are not preloaded

enum_values.name: Preloaded
enum_values.description: CCxE, CCxNE and OCxM bits are preloaded

fields.name: CCUS
fields.description: Capture/compare control update selection Note: This bit acts only on channels that have a complementary output.

enum_values.name: Sw
enum_values.description: When capture/compare control bits are preloaded (CCPC=1), they are updated by setting the COMG bit only

enum_values.name: SwOrEdge
enum_values.description: When capture/compare control bits are preloaded (CCPC=1), they are updated by setting the COMG bit or when an rising edge occurs on TRGI

fields.name: CCDS
fields.description: Capture/compare DMA selection

enum_values.name: OnCompare
enum_values.description: CCx DMA request sent when CCx event occurs

enum_values.name: OnUpdate
enum_values.description: CCx DMA request sent when update event occurs

fields.name: MMS
fields.description: MMS[2:0]: Master mode selection These bits select the information to be sent in master mode to slave timers for synchronization (tim_trgo). The combination is as follows: Other codes reserved Note: The clock of the slave timer or ADC must be enabled prior to receive events from the master timer, and must not be changed on-the-fly while triggers are received from the master timer.

fields.name: TI1S
fields.description: tim_ti1 selection

enum_values.name: Normal
enum_values.description: The TIMx_CH1 pin is connected to TI1 input

enum_values.name: XOR
enum_values.description: The TIMx_CH1, CH2, CH3 pins are connected to TI1 input

fields.name: OIS%s
fields.description: Output Idle state (OC%s output)

enum_values.name: Reset
enum_values.description: OCx=0 (after a dead-time if OCx(N) is implemented) when MOE=0

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: OCx=1 (after a dead-time if OCx(N) is implemented) when MOE=0

fields.name: OIS%sN
fields.description: Output Idle state (OC%sN output)

enum_values.name: Reset
enum_values.description: OCxN=0 after a dead-time when MOE=0

enum_values.name: Set
enum_values.description: OCxN=1 after a dead-time when MOE=0

fields.name: MMS2
fields.description: Master mode selection 2 These bits allow the information to be sent to ADC for synchronization (tim_trgo2) to be selected. The combination is as follows: Note: The clock of the slave timer or ADC must be enabled prior to receive events from the master timer, and must not be changed on-the-fly while triggers are received from the master timer.

fields.name: MMS_3
fields.description: MMS[3]

fields.name: SMS
fields.description: SMS[2:0]: Slave mode selection

fields.name: OCCS
fields.description: OCREF clear selection This bit is used to select the OCREF clear source.

fields.name: TS
fields.description: TS[2:0]: Trigger selection This bitfield is combined with TS[4:3] bits. This bit-field selects the trigger input to be used to synchronize the counter. Others: Reserved See Table 605: TIMx internal trigger connection for more details on tim_itrx meaning for each Timer. Note: These bits must be changed only when they are not used (e.g. when SMS=000) to avoid wrong edge detections at the transition.

fields.name: MSM
fields.description: Master/slave mode

enum_values.name: Sync
enum_values.description: The effect of an event on the trigger input (TRGI) is delayed to allow a perfect synchronization between the current timer and its slaves (through TRGO). It is useful if we want to synchronize several timers on a single external event.

fields.name: ETF
fields.description: External trigger filter This bit-field then defines the frequency used to sample tim_etrp signal and the length of the digital filter applied to tim_etrp. The digital filter is made of an event counter in which N consecutive events are needed to validate a transition on the output:

enum_values.name: NoFilter
enum_values.description: No filter, sampling is done at fDTS

fields.name: ETPS
fields.description: External trigger prescaler External trigger signal tim_etrp frequency must be at most 1/4 of TIMxCLK frequency. A prescaler can be enabled to reduce tim_etrp frequency. It is useful when inputting fast external clocks on tim_etr_in.

fields.name: ECE
fields.description: External clock enable This bit enables External clock mode 2. Note: Setting the ECE bit has the same effect as selecting external clock mode 1 with tim_trgi connected to tim_etrf (SMS=111 and TS=00111). It is possible to simultaneously use external clock mode 2 with the following slave modes: reset mode, gated mode and trigger mode. Nevertheless, tim_trgi must not be connected to tim_etrf in this case (TS bits must not be 00111). Note: If external clock mode 1 and external clock mode 2 are enabled at the same time, the external clock input is tim_etrf.

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: External clock mode 2 enabled. The counter is clocked by any active edge on the ETRF signal.

fields.name: ETP
fields.description: External trigger polarity This bit selects whether tim_etr_in or tim_etr_in is used for trigger operations

enum_values.name: NotInverted
enum_values.description: ETR is noninverted, active at high level or rising edge

enum_values.name: Inverted
enum_values.description: ETR is inverted, active at low level or falling edge

fields.name: SMS_3
fields.description: SMS[3]

fields.name: SMSPE
fields.description: SMS preload enable This bit selects whether the SMS[3:0] bitfield is preloaded

fields.name: SMSPS
fields.description: SMS preload source This bit selects whether the events that triggers the SMS[3:0] bitfield transfer from preload to active

enum_values.name: Update
enum_values.description: SMSM[3:0] is preloaded from Update event

enum_values.name: Index
enum_values.description: SMSM[3:0] is preloaded from Index event

fields.name: UIE
fields.description: Update interrupt enable

fields.name: CC%sIE
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s interrupt enable

fields.name: COMIE
fields.description: COM interrupt enable

fields.name: TIE
fields.description: Trigger interrupt enable

fields.name: BIE
fields.description: Break interrupt enable

fields.name: UDE
fields.description: Update DMA request enable

fields.name: CC%sDE
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s DMA request enable

fields.name: COMDE
fields.description: COM DMA request enable

fields.name: TDE
fields.description: Trigger DMA request enable

fields.name: IDXIE
fields.description: Index interrupt enable

fields.name: DIRIE
fields.description: Direction change interrupt enable

fields.name: IERRIE
fields.description: Index error interrupt enable

fields.name: TERRIE
fields.description: Transition error interrupt enable

fields.name: UIF
fields.description: Update interrupt flag This bit is set by hardware on an update event. It is cleared by software. At overflow or underflow regarding the repetition counter value (update if repetition counter = 0) and if the UDIS=0 in the TIMx_CR1 register. When CNT is reinitialized by software using the UG bit in TIMx_EGR register, if URS=0 and UDIS=0 in the TIMx_CR1 register. When CNT is reinitialized by a trigger event (refer to Section 65.6.3: TIM1 slave mode control register (TIM1_SMCR)), if URS=0 and UDIS=0 in the TIMx_CR1 register.

fields.name: CC%sIF
fields.description: Capture/compare %s interrupt flag

enum_values.name: Match
enum_values.description: If CC1 is an output: The content of the counter TIMx_CNT matches the content of the TIMx_CCR1 register. If CC1 is an input: The counter value has been captured in TIMx_CCR1 register.

fields.name: COMIF
fields.description: COM interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware on COM event (when capture/compare Control bits - CCxE, CCxNE, OCxM - have been updated). It is cleared by software.

fields.name: TIF
fields.description: Trigger interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware on the TRG trigger event (active edge detected on tim_trgi input when the slave mode controller is enabled in all modes but gated mode. It is set when the counter starts or stops when gated mode is selected. It is cleared by software.

fields.name: BIF
fields.description: Break interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware as soon as the break input goes active. It can be cleared by software if the break input is not active.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: An active level has been detected on the break input. An interrupt is generated if BIE=1 in the TIMx_DIER register

fields.name: B2IF
fields.description: Break 2 interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware as soon as the break 2 input goes active. It can be cleared by software if the break 2 input is not active.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: An active level has been detected on the break 2 input. An interrupt is generated if BIE=1 in the TIMx_DIER register

fields.name: CC%sOF
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s overcapture flag

enum_values.name: Overcapture
enum_values.description: The counter value has been captured in TIMx_CCRx register while CCxIF flag was already set

fields.name: SBIF
fields.description: System break interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware as soon as the system break input goes active. It can be cleared by software if the system break input is not active. This flag must be reset to re-start PWM operation.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: An active level has been detected on the system break input. An interrupt is generated if BIE=1 in the TIMx_DIER register

fields.name: CC5IF
fields.description: Compare 5 interrupt flag Refer to CC1IF description Note: Channel 5 can only be configured as output.

fields.name: CC6IF
fields.description: Compare 6 interrupt flag Refer to CC1IF description Note: Channel 6 can only be configured as output.

fields.name: IDXF
fields.description: Index interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware when an index event is detected. It is cleared by software by writing it to '0'.

fields.name: DIRF
fields.description: Direction change interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware when the direction changes in encoder mode (DIR bit value in TIMx_CR is changing). It is cleared by software by writing it to '0'.

enum_values.name: NoTrigger
enum_values.description: No direction change has been detected

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: A direction change has been detected

fields.name: IERRF
fields.description: Index error interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware when an index error is detected. It is cleared by software by writing it to '0'.

enum_values.name: NoTrigger
enum_values.description: No index error has been detected

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: An index erorr has been detected

fields.name: TERRF
fields.description: Transition error interrupt flag This flag is set by hardware when a transition error is detected in encoder mode. It is cleared by software by writing it to '0'.

enum_values.name: NoTrigger
enum_values.description: No encoder transition error has been detected

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: An encoder transition error has been detected

fields.name: UG
fields.description: Update generation This bit can be set by software, it is automatically cleared by hardware.

enum_values.name: Update
enum_values.description: Re-initializes the timer counter and generates an update of the registers.

fields.name: CC%sG
fields.description: Capture/compare %s generation

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: If CC1 is an output: CC1IF flag is set, Corresponding interrupt or DMA request is sent if enabled. If CC1 is an input: The current value of the counter is captured in TIMx_CCR1 register.

fields.name: COMG
fields.description: Capture/compare control update generation This bit can be set by software, it is automatically cleared by hardware Note: This bit acts only on channels having a complementary output.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: When CCPC bit is set, it allows CCxE, CCxNE and OCxM bits to be updated

fields.name: TG
fields.description: Trigger generation This bit is set by software in order to generate an event, it is automatically cleared by hardware.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: The TIF flag is set in TIMx_SR register. Related interrupt or DMA transfer can occur if enabled.

fields.name: BG
fields.description: Break generation This bit is set by software in order to generate an event, it is automatically cleared by hardware.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: A break event is generated. MOE bit is cleared and BIF flag is set. Related interrupt or DMA transfer can occur if enabled

fields.name: B2G
fields.description: Break 2 generation This bit is set by software in order to generate an event, it is automatically cleared by hardware.

enum_values.name: Trigger
enum_values.description: A break 2 event is generated. MOE bit is cleared and B2IF flag is set. Related interrupt can occur if enabled

fields.name: CC1S
fields.description: Capture/compare 1 Selection This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/output) as well as the used input. Note: CC1S bits are writable only when the channel is OFF (CC1E = '0' in TIMx_CCER).

enum_values.name: TI1
enum_values.description: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TI1

enum_values.name: TI2
enum_values.description: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TI2

enum_values.name: TRC
enum_values.description: CC1 channel is configured as input, IC1 is mapped on TRC

fields.name: IC%sPSC
fields.description: Input capture %s prescaler

enum_values.name: NoPrescaler
enum_values.description: No prescaler, capture is done each time an edge is detected on the capture input

enum_values.name: TwoEvents
enum_values.description: Capture is done once every 2 events

enum_values.name: FourEvents
enum_values.description: Capture is done once every 4 events

enum_values.name: EightEvents
enum_values.description: Capture is done once every 8 events

fields.name: IC%sF
fields.description: Input capture %s filter

enum_values.name: NoFilter
enum_values.description: No filter, sampling is done at fDTS

fields.name: CC2S
fields.description: Capture/compare 2 selection This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/output) as well as the used input. Note: CC2S bits are writable only when the channel is OFF (CC2E = '0' in TIMx_CCER).

enum_values.name: TI2
enum_values.description: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TI2

enum_values.name: TI1
enum_values.description: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TI1

enum_values.name: TRC
enum_values.description: CC2 channel is configured as input, IC2 is mapped on TRC

fields.name: CC%sS
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s selection

enum_values.name: Output
enum_values.description: CCx channel is configured as output

fields.name: OC%sFE
fields.description: Output compare %s fast enable

fields.name: OC%sPE
fields.description: Output compare %s preload enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Preload register on CCRx disabled. New values written to CCRx are taken into account immediately

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Preload register on CCRx enabled. Preload value is loaded into active register on each update event

fields.name: OC%sM
fields.description: Output compare %s mode

enum_values.name: Frozen
enum_values.description: The comparison between the output compare register TIMx_CCRy and the counter TIMx_CNT has no effect on the outputs / OpmMode1: Retriggerable OPM mode 1 - In up-counting mode, the channel is active until a trigger event is detected (on TRGI signal). In down-counting mode, the channel is inactive

enum_values.name: ActiveOnMatch
enum_values.description: Set channel to active level on match. OCyREF signal is forced high when the counter matches the capture/compare register / OpmMode2: Inversely to OpmMode1

enum_values.name: InactiveOnMatch
enum_values.description: Set channel to inactive level on match. OCyREF signal is forced low when the counter matches the capture/compare register / Reserved

enum_values.name: ForceInactive
enum_values.description: OCyREF is forced low / CombinedPwmMode1: OCyREF has the same behavior as in PWM mode 1. OCyREFC is the logical OR between OC1REF and OC2REF

enum_values.name: ForceActive
enum_values.description: OCyREF is forced high / CombinedPwmMode2: OCyREF has the same behavior as in PWM mode 2. OCyREFC is the logical AND between OC1REF and OC2REF

enum_values.name: PwmMode1
enum_values.description: In upcounting, channel is active as long as TIMx_CNT<TIMx_CCRy else inactive. In downcounting, channel is inactive as long as TIMx_CNT>TIMx_CCRy else active / AsymmetricPwmMode1: OCyREF has the same behavior as in PWM mode 1. OCyREFC outputs OC1REF when the counter is counting up, OC2REF when it is counting down

enum_values.name: PwmMode2
enum_values.description: Inversely to PwmMode1 / AsymmetricPwmMode2: Inversely to AsymmetricPwmMode1

fields.name: OC%sCE
fields.description: Output compare %s clear enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: OCxRef is not affected by the ocref_clr_int signal

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: OCxRef is cleared as soon as a High level is detected on ocref_clr_int signal

fields.name: OC%sM_3
fields.description: Output compare %s mode, bit 3

fields.name: CC3S
fields.description: Capture/compare 3 selection This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/output) as well as the used input. Note: CC3S bits are writable only when the channel is OFF (CC3E = '0' in TIMx_CCER).

enum_values.name: TI3
enum_values.description: CC3 channel is configured as input, IC3 is mapped on TI3

enum_values.name: TI4
enum_values.description: CC3 channel is configured as input, IC3 is mapped on TI4

enum_values.name: TRC
enum_values.description: CC3 channel is configured as input, IC3 is mapped on TRC

fields.name: IC%sPSC
fields.description: Input capture %s prescaler

fields.name: IC%sF
fields.description: Input capture %s filter

fields.name: CC4S
fields.description: Capture/compare 4 selection This bit-field defines the direction of the channel (input/output) as well as the used input. Note: CC4S bits are writable only when the channel is OFF (CC4E = '0' in TIMx_CCER).

enum_values.name: TI4
enum_values.description: CC4 channel is configured as input, IC4 is mapped on TI4

enum_values.name: TI3
enum_values.description: CC4 channel is configured as input, IC4 is mapped on TI3

enum_values.name: TRC
enum_values.description: CC4 channel is configured as input, IC4 is mapped on TRC

fields.name: CC%sS
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s selection

fields.name: OC%sFE
fields.description: Output compare %s fast enable

fields.name: OC%sPE
fields.description: Output compare %s preload enable

fields.name: OC%sM
fields.description: Output compare %s mode

fields.name: OC%sCE
fields.description: Output compare %s clear enable

fields.name: OC%sM_3
fields.description: Output compare %s mode, bit 3

fields.name: CC%sE
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s output enable

fields.name: CC%sP
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s output Polarity

fields.name: CC%sNE
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s complementary output enable

fields.name: CC%sNP
fields.description: Capture/Compare %s output Polarity

fields.name: CNT
fields.description: Counter value Non-dithering mode (DITHEN = 0) The register holds the counter value. Dithering mode (DITHEN = 1) The register only holds the non-dithered part in CNT[15:0]. The fractional part is not available.

fields.name: UIFCPY
fields.description: UIF copy This bit is a read-only copy of the UIF bit of the TIMx_ISR register. If the UIFREMAP bit in the TIMxCR1 is reset, bit 31 is reserved and read at 0.

fields.name: PSC
fields.description: Prescaler value The counter clock frequency (f tim_cnt_ck ) is equal to f tim_psc_ck / (PSC[15:0] + 1). PSC contains the value to be loaded in the active prescaler register at each update event (including when the counter is cleared through UG bit of TIMx_EGR register or through trigger controller when configured in 'reset mode').

fields.name: ARR
fields.description: Auto-reload value ARR is the value to be loaded in the actual auto-reload register. Refer to the Section 65.3.3: Time-base unit on page 4457 for more details about ARR update and behavior. The counter is blocked while the auto-reload value is null. Non-dithering mode (DITHEN = 0) The register holds the auto-reload value. Dithering mode (DITHEN = 1) The register holds the integer part in ARR[19:4]. The ARR[3:0] bitfield contains the dithered part.

fields.name: REP
fields.description: Repetition counter reload value This bitfield defines the update rate of the compare registers (i.e. periodic transfers from preload to active registers) when preload registers are enable. It also defines the update interrupt generation rate, if this interrupt is enable. When the repetition down-counter reaches zero, an update event is generated and it restarts counting from REP value. As the repetition counter is reloaded with REP value only at the repetition update event UEV, any write to the TIMx_RCR register is not taken in account until the next repetition update event. It means in PWM mode (REP+1) corresponds to: the number of PWM periods in edge-aligned mode the number of half PWM period in center-aligned mode.

fields.name: CCR
fields.description: Capture/Compare value

fields.name: DTG
fields.description: Dead-time generator setup This bit-field defines the duration of the dead-time inserted between the complementary outputs. DT correspond to this duration. DTG[7:5]=0xx = DT=DTG[7:0]x t dtg with t dtg =t DTS . DTG[7:5]=10x = DT=(64+DTG[5:0])xt dtg with T dtg =2xt DTS . DTG[7:5]=110 = DT=(32+DTG[4:0])xt dtg with T dtg =8xt DTS . DTG[7:5]=111 = DT=(32+DTG[4:0])xt dtg with T dtg =16xt DTS . Example if T DTS =125ns (8MHz), dead-time possible values are: 0 to 15875 ns by 125 ns steps, 16 us to 31750 ns by 250 ns steps, 32 us to 63us by 1 us steps, 64 us to 126 us by 2 us steps Note: This bit-field can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1, 2 or 3 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: LOCK
fields.description: Lock configuration These bits offer a write protection against software errors. Note: The LOCK bits can be written only once after the reset. Once the TIMx_BDTR register has been written, their content is frozen until the next reset.

enum_values.name: Level1
enum_values.description: Any bits except MOE, OSSR, OSSI and LOCK in TIMx_BDTR register, OISx and OISxN bits in TIMx_CR2 register can no longer be written

enum_values.name: Level2
enum_values.description: LOCK Level 1 + CC Polarity bits (CCxP/CCxNP bits in TIMx_CCER register, as long as the related channel is configured in output through the CCxS bits) as well as OSSR and OSSI bits can no longer be written

enum_values.name: Level3
enum_values.description: LOCK Level 2 + CC Control bits (OCxM and OCxPE bits in TIMx_CCMRx registers, as long as the related channel is configured in output through the CCxS bits) can no longer be written

fields.name: OSSI
fields.description: Off-state selection for idle mode This bit is used when MOE=0 due to a break event or by a software write, on channels configured as outputs. See OC/OCN enable description for more details (Section 65.6.11: TIM1 capture/compare enable register (TIM1_CCER)). Note: This bit can not be modified as soon as the LOCK level 2 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

enum_values.name: HiZ
enum_values.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are disabled

enum_values.name: IdleLevel
enum_values.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are forced to idle level

fields.name: OSSR
fields.description: Off-state selection for Run mode This bit is used when MOE=1 on channels having a complementary output which are configured as outputs. OSSR is not implemented if no complementary output is implemented in the timer. See OC/OCN enable description for more details (Section 65.6.11: TIM1 capture/compare enable register (TIM1_CCER)). Note: This bit can not be modified as soon as the LOCK level 2 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

enum_values.name: HiZ
enum_values.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are disabled

enum_values.name: IdleLevel
enum_values.description: When inactive, OC/OCN outputs are enabled with their inactive level

fields.name: BKE
fields.description: Break enable This bit enables the complete break protection (including all sources connected to bk_acth and BKIN sources, as per Figure 635: Break and Break2 circuitry overview). Note: This bit cannot be modified when LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register). Note: Any write operation to this bit takes a delay of 1 APB clock cycle to become effective.

fields.name: BKP
fields.description: Break polarity Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register). Note: Any write operation to this bit takes a delay of 1 APB clock cycle to become effective.

enum_values.name: ActiveLow
enum_values.description: Break input BRKx is active low

enum_values.name: ActiveHigh
enum_values.description: Break input BRKx is active high

fields.name: AOE
fields.description: Automatic output enable Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

enum_values.name: Manual
enum_values.description: MOE can be set only by software

enum_values.name: Automatic
enum_values.description: MOE can be set by software or automatically at the next update event (if none of the break inputs BRK and BRK2 is active)

fields.name: MOE
fields.description: Main output enable This bit is cleared asynchronously by hardware as soon as one of the break inputs is active (tim_brk or tim_brk2). It is set by software or automatically depending on the AOE bit. It is acting only on the channels which are configured in output. In response to a break event or if MOE is written to 0: OC and OCN outputs are disabled or forced to idle state depending on the OSSI bit. See OC/OCN enable description for more details (Section 65.6.11: TIM1 capture/compare enable register (TIM1_CCER)).

enum_values.name: DisabledIdle
enum_values.description: OC/OCN are disabled or forced idle depending on OSSI

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: OC/OCN are enabled if CCxE/CCxNE are set

fields.name: BKF
fields.description: Break filter This bit-field defines the frequency used to sample tim_brk input and the length of the digital filter applied to tim_brk. The digital filter is made of an event counter in which N consecutive events are needed to validate a transition on the output: Note: This bit cannot be modified when LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

enum_values.name: NoFilter
enum_values.description: No filter, sampling is done at fDTS

fields.name: BK2F
fields.description: Break 2 filter This bit-field defines the frequency used to sample tim_brk2 input and the length of the digital filter applied to tim_brk2. The digital filter is made of an event counter in which N consecutive events are needed to validate a transition on the output: Note: This bit cannot be modified when LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BK2E
fields.description: Break 2 enable This bit enables the complete break 2 protection (including all sources connected to bk_acth and BKIN sources, as per Figure 635: Break and Break2 circuitry overview). Note: The BRKIN2 must only be used with OSSR = OSSI = 1. Note: This bit cannot be modified when LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register). Note: Any write operation to this bit takes a delay of 1 APB clock cycle to become effective.

fields.name: BK2P
fields.description: Break 2 polarity Note: This bit cannot be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register). Note: Any write operation to this bit takes a delay of 1 APB clock cycle to become effective.

fields.name: BKDSRM
fields.description: Break disarm This bit is cleared by hardware when no break source is active. The BKDSRM bit must be set by software to release the bidirectional output control (open-drain output in Hi-Z state) and then be polled it until it is reset by hardware, indicating that the fault condition has disappeared. Note: Any write operation to this bit takes a delay of 1 APB clock cycle to become effective.

fields.name: BK2DSRM
fields.description: Break2 disarm Refer to BKDSRM description

fields.name: BKBID
fields.description: Break bidirectional In the bidirectional mode (BKBID bit set to 1), the break input is configured both in input mode and in open drain output mode. Any active break event asserts a low logic level on the Break input to indicate an internal break event to external devices. Note: This bit cannot be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register). Note: Any write operation to this bit takes a delay of 1 APB clock cycle to become effective.

enum_values.name: Input
enum_values.description: Break input BRK in input mode

enum_values.name: Bidirectional
enum_values.description: Break input BRK in bidirectional mode

fields.name: BK2BID
fields.description: Break2 bidirectional Refer to BKBID description

enum_values.name: Input
enum_values.description: Break input BRK2 in input mode

enum_values.name: Bidirectional
enum_values.description: Break input BRK2 in bidirectional mode

fields.name: CCR
fields.description: Capture/Compare value

fields.name: GC5C1
fields.description: Group channel 5 and channel 1 Distortion on channel 1 output: This bit can either have immediate effect or be preloaded and taken into account after an update event (if preload feature is selected in TIMxCCMR1). Note: it is also possible to apply this distortion on combined PWM signals.

fields.name: GC5C2
fields.description: Group channel 5 and channel 2 Distortion on channel 2 output: This bit can either have immediate effect or be preloaded and taken into account after an update event (if preload feature is selected in TIMxCCMR1). Note: it is also possible to apply this distortion on combined PWM signals.

fields.name: GC5C3
fields.description: Group channel 5 and channel 3 Distortion on channel 3 output: This bit can either have immediate effect or be preloaded and taken into account after an update event (if preload feature is selected in TIMxCCMR2). Note: it is also possible to apply this distortion on combined PWM signals.

fields.name: CCR
fields.description: Capture/Compare value

fields.name: OC%sFE
fields.description: Output compare %s fast enable

fields.name: OC%sPE
fields.description: Output compare %s preload enable

fields.name: OC%sM
fields.description: Output compare %s mode

fields.name: OC%sCE
fields.description: Output compare %s clear enable

fields.name: OC%sM_3
fields.description: Output compare %s mode, bit 3

fields.name: DTGF
fields.description: Dead-time falling edge generator setup This bit-field defines the duration of the dead-time inserted between the complementary outputs, on the falling edge. DTGF[7:5]=0xx = DTF=DTGF[7:0]x t dtg with t dtg =t DTS . DTGF[7:5]=10x = DTF=(64+DTGF[5:0])xt dtg with T dtg =2xt DTS . DTGF[7:5]=110 = DTF=(32+DTGF[4:0])xt dtg with T dtg =8xt DTS . DTGF[7:5]=111 = DTF=(32+DTGF[4:0])xt dtg with T dtg =16xt DTS . Example if T DTS =125ns (8MHz), dead-time possible values are: 0 to 15875 ns by 125 ns steps, 16 us to 31750 ns by 250 ns steps, 32 us to 63us by 1 us steps, 64 us to 126 us by 2 us steps Note: This bit-field can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1, 2 or 3 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: DTAE
fields.description: Deadtime asymmetric enable Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1, 2 or 3 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: DTPE
fields.description: Deadtime preload enable Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1, 2 or 3 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: IE
fields.description: Index enable This bit indicates if the Index event resets the counter.

fields.name: IDIR
fields.description: Index direction This bit indicates in which direction the Index event resets the counter. Note: The IDR[1:0] bitfield must be written when IE bit is reset (index disabled).

fields.name: IBLK
fields.description: Index blanking This bit indicates if the Index event is conditioned by the tim_ti3 input

fields.name: FIDX
fields.description: First index This bit indicates if the first index only is taken into account

fields.name: IPOS
fields.description: Index positioning In quadrature encoder mode (SMS[3:0] = 0001, 0010, 0011, 1110, 1111), this bit indicates in which AB input configuration the Index event resets the counter. In directional clock mode or clock plus direction mode (SMS[3:0] = 1010, 1011, 1100, 1101), these bits indicates on which level the Index event resets the counter. In bidirectional clock mode, this applies for both clock inputs. x0: Index resets the counter when clock is 0 x1: Index resets the counter when clock is 1 Note: IPOS[1] bit is not significant

fields.name: PW
fields.description: Pulse width This bitfield defines the pulse duration, as following: t PW = PW[7:0] x t PWG

fields.name: PWPRSC
fields.description: Pulse width prescaler This bitfield sets the clock prescaler for the pulse generator, as following: t PWG = (2sup(PWPRSC[2:0])/sup) x t tim_ker_ck

fields.name: TI1SEL
fields.description: Selects tim_ti1[0..15] input ... Refer to Section 65.3.2: TIM1 pins and internal signals for interconnects list.

fields.name: TI2SEL
fields.description: Selects tim_ti2[0..15] input ... Refer to Section 65.3.2: TIM1 pins and internal signals for interconnects list.

fields.name: TI3SEL
fields.description: Selects tim_ti3[0..15] input ... Refer to Section 65.3.2: TIM1 pins and internal signals for interconnects list.

fields.name: TI4SEL
fields.description: Selects tim_ti4[0..15] input ... Refer to Section 65.3.2: TIM1 pins and internal signals for interconnects list.

fields.name: BKINE
fields.description: TIMx_BKIN input enable This bit enables the TIMx_BKIN alternate function input for the timer's tim_brk input. TIMx_BKIN input is 'ORed' with the other tim_brk sources. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BKCMP1E
fields.description: tim_brk_cmp1 enable This bit enables the tim_brk_cmp1 for the timer's tim_brk input. tim_brk_cmp1 output is 'ORed' with the other tim_brk sources. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BKCMP8E
fields.description: tim_brk_cmp8 enable This bit enables the tim_brk_cmp8 for the timer's tim_brk input. tim_brk_cmp8 output is 'ORed' with the other tim_brk sources. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BKINP
fields.description: TIMx_BKIN input polarity This bit selects the TIMx_BKIN alternate function input sensitivity. It must be programmed together with the BKP polarity bit. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BKCMP1P
fields.description: tim_brk_cmp1 input polarity This bit selects the tim_brk_cmp1 input sensitivity. It must be programmed together with the BKP polarity bit. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BKCMP2P

fields.name: ETRSEL
fields.description: etr_in source selection These bits select the etr_in input source. ... Refer to Section 65.3.2: TIM1 pins and internal signals for product specific implementation. Note: These bits can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BK2INE

Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.description: tim_brk2_cmp7 enable This bit enables the tim_brk2_cmp7 for the timer's tim_brk2 input. tim_brk2_cmp7 output is 'ORed' with the other tim_brk2 sources. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BK2CMP8E
fields.description: tim_brk2_cmp8 enable This bit enables the tim_brk2_cmp8 for the timer's tim_brk2 input. tim_brk2_cmp8 output is 'ORed' with the other tim_brk2 sources. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BK2INP
fields.description: TIMx_BKIN2 input polarity This bit selects the TIMx_BKIN2 alternate function input sensitivity. It must be programmed together with the BK2P polarity bit. Note: This bit can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: BK2CMP1P

fields.name: OCRSEL
fields.description: ocref_clr source selection These bits select the ocref_clr input source. ... Refer to Section 65.3.2: TIM1 pins and internal signals for product specific information. Note: These bits can not be modified as long as LOCK level 1 has been programmed (LOCK bits in TIMx_BDTR register).

fields.name: DBA
fields.description: DMA base address This 5-bits vector defines the base-address for DMA transfers (when read/write access are done through the TIMx_DMAR address). DBA is defined as an offset starting from the address of the TIMx_CR1 register. Example: ...

fields.name: DBL
fields.description: DMA burst length This 5-bit vector defines the length of DMA transfers (the timer recognizes a burst transfer when a read or a write access is done to the TIMx_DMAR address), i.e. the number of transfers. Transfers can be in half-words or in bytes (see example below). ... Example: Let us consider the following transfer: DBL = 7 bytes & DBA = TIM2_CR1. If DBL = 7 bytes and DBA = TIM2_CR1 represents the address of the byte to be transferred, the address of the transfer should be given by the following equation: (TIMx_CR1 address) + DBA + (DMA index), where DMA index = DBL In this example, 7 bytes are added to (TIMx_CR1 address) + DBA, which gives us the address from/to which the data are copied. In this case, the transfer is done to 7 registers starting from the following address: (TIMx_CR1 address) + DBA According to the configuration of the DMA Data Size, several cases may occur: If the DMA Data Size is configured in half-words, 16-bit data are transferred to each of the 7 registers. If the DMA Data Size is configured in bytes, the data are also transferred to 7 registers: the first register contains the first MSB byte, the second register, the first LSB byte and so on. So with the transfer Timer, one also has to specify the size of data transferred by DMA.

fields.name: DBSS
fields.description: DMA burst source selection This bitfield defines the interrupt source that triggers the DMA burst transfers (the timer recognizes a burst transfer when a read or a write access is done to the TIMx_DMAR address). Others: reserved

registers.name: DMAR
registers.description: TIM1 DMA address for full transfer

fields.name: DMAB
fields.description: DMA register for burst accesses A read or write operation to the DMAR register accesses the register located at the address (TIMx_CR1 address) + (DBA + DMA index) x 4 where TIMx_CR1 address is the address of the control register 1, DBA is the DMA base address configured in TIMx_DCR register, DMA index is automatically controlled by the DMA transfer, and ranges from 0 to DBL (DBL configured in TIMx_DCR).
